title: Boat Rental in Tenerife: Costs, Spots and Tips for 2026
html:
Boat rental in Tenerife puts the Atlantic and resident pilot whales within easy reach, with most charters leaving Puerto Colon in Costa Adeje. Expect a day price band of roughly EUR 350 to EUR 2,500 depending on the boat, and BoatHire24 lists skippered options across the island.

What can you do by boat in Tenerife?

The headline activity is watching the resident pods of short-finned pilot whales and bottlenose dolphins that live year-round off the south-west coast. Beyond the wildlife, a half-day charter pairs a cliff cruise with a swim stop in a sheltered cove, and many visitors anchor for lunch and snorkel over the clear shelves.

Where to set off in Tenerife

The south-west holds the densest cluster of charter berths, with calmer water and shorter runs to the whale grounds. These departure points are reliable starting marinas and harbours:

  • Puerto Colon in Costa Adeje, the busiest charter base on the island.
  • Puerto Deportivo Los Gigantes, set beneath the Acantilados de los Gigantes cliffs.
  • Marina del Sur at Las Galletas, handy for the quieter eastern coves.
  • Puerto de Los Cristianos, a working harbour with day berths.
  • Radazul near Santa Cruz, on the greener north-east shoulder.

From Puerto Colon it is only a short hop, well under five nautical miles, to the calm channel where the pilot whales gather.

Which boat to rent in Tenerife

Your choice comes down to group size, comfort and how far you want to range along the coast. Smaller motorboats suit short hops, while a flybridge motor yacht earns its keep on a full day.

  1. Sailing catamaran: best for stable, shaded family days; seats around 10 to 12 guests; from EUR 450.
  2. RIB or small motorboat: best for quick whale runs and coves; seats 6 to 8 guests; from EUR 300.
  3. Motor yacht: best for a comfortable full day with a saloon to retreat into; seats up to 12 guests; from EUR 900. For a flybridge layout that holds a group easily you can compare the Fairline.
  4. Sailing yacht: best for a quieter, wind-led cruise; seats around 8 guests; from EUR 500.
  5. Crewed superyacht: best for a premium private day with full service; seats 10 to 12 guests; from EUR 2,000.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Tenerife?

Prices scale with the size of the boat, the day length and whether a skipper is included. As a guide for the 2026 season, the bands below cover the common options:

  • A shared whale-watching tour ticket costs from about EUR 25 to EUR 45 per adult.
  • A small private motorboat for a half-day starts near EUR 300 in Tenerife.
  • A catamaran for a full day runs from roughly EUR 450 to EUR 1,200.
  • A crewed yacht for a private full day starts around EUR 2,000 and rises with size.

For firm numbers it is quickest to book a boat online, where each listing shows the skipper, fuel policy and capacity before you commit.

How does booking a charter work?

You pick your dates, reserve the boat, and the operator confirms the berth and what is included. Most Tenerife listings state whether fuel, a skipper and snorkelling gear come as standard, so read the inclusions before you pay.

If you are weighing several boats at once, you can search available boats and filter by guests, marina and date.

When is the best season, and do you need a licence?

Tenerife charters run all year, but the warmest, calmest stretch falls between June and September. The trade winds, known locally as the alisios, freshen most afternoons and push a swell along the exposed north, so the sheltered south-west sees the bulk of charter traffic. Spain lets visitors ride as guests on a skippered boat without any qualification, though the operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat.

For the steadiest sea and the best whale sightings, board before midday from Puerto Colon: the morning water is glassy, the alisios pick up after lunch, and the pilot whales sit close to the surface in the calm channel between Tenerife and La Gomera.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 300 to EUR 2,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 3 to 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 6 to 12 on most boats
  • Currency: EUR (euro)
  • Main base: Puerto Colon, Costa Adeje

Questions about boat rental in Tenerife

Can you see whales on a boat trip in Tenerife?

Yes, the south-west waters hold resident pilot whales and bottlenose dolphins all year, so sightings are common on a short cruise from Puerto Colon, though wild animals are never guaranteed on any single trip.

How many people fit on a charter boat?

Most private charter boats in Tenerife carry between six and twelve guests, with catamarans and larger motor yachts at the upper end; the listing always states the legal capacity for that specific vessel.

Is a half-day or full-day trip better?

A half-day of three to four hours suits whale watching and a single swim stop, while a full day of seven to eight hours lets you reach Los Gigantes, anchor for lunch and snorkel at a slower pace.

Are charters in Tenerife skippered?

The majority are skippered, meaning a licensed captain drives the boat while you relax, which suits Atlantic water; bareboat options exist for qualified renters, and each listing makes the arrangement clear.

Tenerife rewards an early start and a calm southern berth, and BoatHire24 makes it simple to compare verified boats and check availability for your dates. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Tenerife or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Tenerife and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
